methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cascade Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Jacob Portukalian and Arlen Abraham, having raised $5.9M to date. The valuation universe includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though only 1 comparable operates at an adjacent stage with sufficient similarity.

Stage-progression analysis is the only applicable triangulation method given available data. Using total capital raised ($5.9M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to series A, this method implies a $15M valuation. Peer-median analysis cannot be applied—while 23 seed or near-seed companies exist in Space & Aerospace, zero meet narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent valuation tier, similar profile), rendering a median unreliable. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable; the sector median P/S is 64.2x across the five public tickers, but Cascade's revenue is not disclosed, precluding revenue-based valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, centered at $15M, with substantial uncertainty acknowledged. The single-method foundation reflects both the nascent stage and sparse comparable data at seed in this capital-intensive sector. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier—which may undervalue if Cascade has differentiated technical capabilities—and potential future revenue trajectory relative to better-capitalized peers.
